name: New Duchess Spice Cake w/ Maple Buttercream F
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease and flour two 9-inch layer cake pans or one 13 x 9-inch oblong pan.
2. Sift together the flour, sugar, baking soda, cinnamon, nutmeg, cloves, and salt.
3. Add the brown sugar, shortening, and buttermilk.
4. Beat for 2 minutes.
5. Add eggs.
6. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
7. Bake layer cake pans for 30 to 35 minutes or oblong pan for 45 minutes until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean.
8. Cool.
MAPLE BUTTERCREAM FROSTING:
1/2 cup Crisco shortening (regular only)
1/2 cup Butter, softened
1 teaspoon Maple flavoring (like vanilla extract)
4 cups (1 lb.) powdered sugar
2 Tablespoons Milk
1. Cream butter and shortening together until light and fluffy.
2. Add maple flavoring.
3. Gradually add powdered sugar, beating for 2 minutes.
4. Gradually add milk, continuing to beat until light and airy. Add more milk if needed.
5. Color with paste colors as desired. Note that any color added will affect the overall yellow tint of the frosting.
This icing is a light yellow and colors you add will be affected by this. For pure white icing, use 1 cup regular Crisco shortening and 1/2 teaspoon Wilton butter flavoring. This icing is somewhat stiffer. For blazing white, add Wilton White White. Wilton White White will also make the first buttercream recipe whiter, but be careful with colors as they will still tend to be yellowish.
Note: Excellent cake and frosting! Cake from the old '50s Betty Crocker Cookbook; Frosting adapted from various sources.
